response.justification: Bogdan brings credible Staff-level experience, strong TypeScript, and — most relevantly — current hands-on work at Stripe building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, which maps directly to the AI-native engineering environment described. Dunnhumby provides genuine high-throughput distributed systems credibility (millions of HTTP requests per second, low-latency async I/O). The critical blocker is Python: it is listed as a must-have skill yet is entirely absent from the resume, skills list, and every role description. Additionally, evidence of technical strategy influence, stakeholder management, and operational ownership (on-call, incident response) is sparse. A short screening call to verify Python proficiency and probe strategy depth is warranted before a full loop.
strengths: Current Stripe role involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development, directly relevant to an AI-native engineering environment
Dunnhumby role demonstrates real high-throughput distributed systems experience: millions of HTTP requests per second, async I/O, low-latency design
Consistent Staff-level framing across multiple companies with evidence of technical breadth and open-source ownership
concerns: Python is a must-have requirement but is completely absent from the resume, all role descriptions, and the skills list
No mention of on-call rotations, incident response, or operational ownership — all explicit requirements of the role
Technical strategy and cross-functional stakeholder management are not demonstrated; roles read as individual contributor execution rather than domain-level direction

key_factors.factor: AI-agent and MCP work at Stripe
